series.title: [PATCH v2] ppc/pnv: generate dtb after machine initialization is

review.summary: The change correctly defers POWERNV DTB generation until machine initialization is complete, which matches the problem statement and avoids building an incomplete DTB before late-created devices exist. Moving the BMC discovery/setup alongside DTB generation is also coherent because the BMC affects the generated tree. I did not identify a functional regression in the reset path from the patch itself.

stages.A: Problem statement and fix direction are aligned: generating the DTB from pnv_init() is too early, and machine-init-done is the right synchronization point for a complete device tree.
stages.B: Code movement is internally consistent. DTB creation is removed from pnv_init(), the reset path still consumes machine->fdt, and the new notifier creates/ packs the DTB once initialization is complete.
stages.C: Risk appears low. The main behavior change is timing of DTB creation and BMC hookup; both are intentionally delayed until all machine devices exist. No obvious lifetime or ownership issue is introduced by embedding the notifier in PnvMachineState.
stages.D: Available validation is limited to the provided metadata: git am succeeded and configure completed. No runtime or functional test results were provided, so boot-time coverage of dumpdtb and POWERNV guests remains an unverified area.
stages.E: No blocking issues found. The patch is suitable to take as-is based on the provided diff.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
